content.problemStatement: Given a Binary Tree, return its Top View. The Top View of a Binary Tree is the set of nodes visible when we see the tree from the top.
constraints: 1 <= Number of nodes in the tree <= 10^5
content.inputFormat: The input will be a binary tree represented as an array where the index represents the node's value and the value at that index represents the node's children. The root of the tree is the first element in the array.
content.outputFormat: The output will be an array representing the top view of the binary tree.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Use a breadth-first search (BFS) algorithm to traverse the tree and keep track of the nodes at each horizontal distance from the root.

code.Python3:
from collections import deque

# Class representing a node in the binary tree
class Node:
    def __init__(self, data):
        self.data = data
        self.left = None
        self.right = None

# Function to get the top view of the binary tree
def topView(root):
    if root is None: return

    # Create a queue for BFS and enqueue the root node
    q = deque([root])

    # Create a dictionary to store the top view nodes
    topViewNodes = {}

    # Perform BFS
    while q:
        current_node = q.popleft()

        # If the current node is not NULL, enqueue its children
        if current_node:
            if current_node.left: q.append(current_node.left)
            if current_node.right: q.append(currentNode.right)

        # If the current node is at the top view, add it to the top view nodes dictionary
        if current_node and (current_node.left is None or current_node.right is None):
            topViewNodes[current_node.data] = current_node.data

    # Print the top view nodes
    for key in topViewNodes:
        print(topViewNodes[key], end=' ')
    print()

# Create the binary tree
root = Node(1)
root.left = Node(2)
root.right = Node(3)
root.left.left = Node(4)
root.left.right = Node(5)
root.right.left = Node(6)
root.right.right = Node(7)

# Get the top view of the binary tree
topView(root)
